title: Add tests for root-dependent tool behavior before and after initialization
summary: Add deterministic tests that verify root-dependent runtime components fail clearly before workspace initialization when startup cwd is '/', then succeed with the bound project root after `workspace_initialize(cwd)` is performed, while preserving the safety invariant that no artifact path resolves under '/.agent'.
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify that root-dependent operations fail with actionable errors before initialization when the server starts with cwd='/' and no repo root is bound.
Tests verify that the same root-dependent operations succeed after `workspace_initialize(cwd)` binds a valid project root.
Tests verify that runtime components consistently use the initialized root for `.agent` artifacts, repo commands, and verify-script execution.
Tests preserve the safety invariant that runtime behavior never resolves canonical or derived artifacts under '/.agent'.
Added tests contribute to keeping overall project coverage at or above 90%.

steps.description: Identify root-dependent runtime paths that should reject execution before initialization, including state persistence, ops helpers, repo helpers, verify execution, and commit flow.
Add tests that assert clear failure behavior when these operations run while repo root is unresolved after cwd='/' startup.
Add tests that bind a valid project root through the new initialization flow and verify the same operations succeed using that bound root.
Add negative safety assertions confirming that no tested path can fall back to '/.agent' or otherwise bypass initialized-root validation.
Run targeted test coverage for the affected runtime components and confirm the new regression tests protect the initialized-root contract.

notes: Keep tests hermetic and based on temporary directories; do not rely on a real Zed process.
Prefer direct assertions on raised error messages, resolved paths, and bound-root behavior over broad end-to-end scaffolding.
This ticket is test-focused and should not introduce unrelated runtime behavior changes.
